top_signals.title: Brands Hope for Big Payoff From World Cup Spending
top_signals.summary: Global spending on the World Cup could top $80 billion. But even though it starts in a few weeks, many hotels in US host cities still have rooms available and games are not sold out because ticket prices are so high. But brands such as Adidas and Nike are still spending millions on partnerships. Bloomberg Intelligence Senior Retail Staples Analyst Jennifer Bartashus talks about it all on Bloomberg Radio. (Source: Bloomberg)
top_signals.why_it_matters: AI and semiconductor developments impacting technology leadership and capex cycles.

top_signals.title: Activist Ancora Pushes H.B. Fuller to Drop Bid for UK’s AMS
top_signals.summary: Activist investor Ancora Holdings Group is pushing US adhesives maker H.B. Fuller Co. to abandon a proposed takeover of UK-based Advanced Medical Solutions Group Plc.
